description: A Class D conduct artifact with values_frame (both optimizing_for and not_optimizing_for), at least one alternative, reasoning_trace, challenge_surface, reversibility_score, calibration block, and a complete commit_chain with preimage content at every stage and dependency linkage between stages MUST receive CONDUCT_VALID status.

input.claim: the recommended strategy is to ship F-1 as a class-scoped governance correction rather than register a second canonicalization scheme in v0.1.0

not_optimizing_for: preservation of existing WISEATA wire format under v0.1.0

alternatives.summary: Register WISEATA-CANONICAL-V1 as a second Class A canonicalization scheme in v0.x+1.
alternatives.rejected_because: Forks canonicalization at genesis and weakens the single-rule guarantee.

alternatives.summary: Migrate WISEATA proofs to JCS-encoded JSON before declaring Class A.
alternatives.rejected_because: Out of scope for a governance-only correction; runtime change is gated on a separate decision.

reasoning_trace.claim: Multiple canonicalization schemes at v0.1.0 would fragment the ecosystem before convergence.
Class-scoped conformance lets WISEATA participate as Class B today without weakening Class A.
Future versions MAY register additional schemes; the v0.1.0 lock costs nothing irreversible.

recommended_action.summary: Reduce WISEATA classes_supported to ['B']; retain Winstack candidate ['A','B']; add JCS-only vector backstop.

challenge_surface.argument: If WISEATA never migrates to JCS, Class A adoption remains thin and the lock looks ideological rather than functional.
Single canonicalization may bias the protocol toward JSON-shaped artifacts at the expense of binary-native formats.
A second scheme registered later still requires §4 enumeration and version negotiation — the cost is deferred, not avoided.

success_signals: ≥2 independent Class A implementations using JCS by review date
no demand for a second canonicalization scheme from external implementers
failure_signals: no Class A implementations achieve conformance
external implementers blocked by JCS requirement

not_optimizing_for: preservation of existing WISEATA wire format under v0.1.0

content.reasoning_trace_summary: single-rule lock at genesis; class-scoped participation; reversible later

content.recommended_action: governance_correction: reduce WISEATA to ['B']; retain Winstack ['A','B']; add JCS-only vector

why: WiseOrder Protocol v0.1.0 §3 Class D: a conduct artifact MUST expose what it optimized for AND what it refused to optimize for (D1), generate ≥1 defensible alternative (D2), include explicit self-generated counterarguments (D3), preserve preimage content at every commit stage (D5/CC1), and link each later stage to its predecessor (CC2/CC4). With all required fields present and the commit chain intact and ordered, status MUST be CONDUCT_VALID. Per D4, Class D MUST NOT receive VERIFIED — only CONDUCT_VALID/CONDUCT_INVALID. Mitigates threat D-1 (post-hoc rationalization). Exercises D1, D2, D3, D4, D5, CC1, CC2, CC4.
